verbatim_text: Now, we can think about this set of operations as some kind of an exploration that's sampled from the algorithm. It's a trace sample from the algorithm. What is this exploration really doing? It's learning about the test problem. It's trying to figure out through search what is the right proof technique for this problem. And we can think about this as some optimization done solely for the problem at test time. So exploration is trying to learn about the test problem. And we can think about an algorithm now that we want that can solve a variety of problems or a variety of test problems. So this algorithm needs to learn to explore or, in other words, learn to learn more about the test problem. And this notion of learning to learn in machine learning is typically referred to as meta learning. And in this context of maximizing terminal rewards, that's nothing but meta reinforcement learning. So we have equated this notion of learning algorithms as equivalent to solving this meta reinforcement learning problem. Now, what does this insight really bias? OK, first, it's a nice conceptual insight.
